topic: Case study: a reproducible imputation & robust-handling pipeline
scope: Inspected, leakage-safe, fitted on train only — not 'hands-off.'
key_concepts: A concrete illustration of tying imputation and outlier management together safely.
Shows how missing indicator generation, model-based imputation, and robust scaling are chained together in a pipeline object.
Emphasizes that this pipeline is fitted solely on the training data, then used to transform the test data, completely avoiding target leakage.
Reiterates that automation in the pipeline requires prior human inspection of the mechanisms (MCAR/MAR) and outlier distributions.
examples: A housing or medical dataset where one feature gets an indicator and mean imputation, while a highly skewed numerical feature gets capped at the 95th percentile, all executed as a single pipeline object.

relations.builds_on: ch05_s02 (Pipeline component theory put into practice)

figures.caption: Skema pipeline penanganan missing values dan outlier yang reproducible
figures.description: A block diagram showing raw split training data going into a pipeline containing an indicator generator, an imputer, and a robust scaler, with the fitted parameters stored and applied identically to the test split.

exclusions: Large blocks of full code. Use the signature placeholder [KODE] instead.
